original_theorem:
Suppose that  $K$ is a bounded set with zero content and $\epsilon,$
$\rho>0.$  Then there are cubes $C_1,$ $C_2,$ \dots$,$
$C_r$ with edge lengths
$<\rho$ such that  $C_j\cap K\ne\emptyset,$ $1\le j\le r,$
\begin{equation}\label{eq:7.3.5}
K\subset\bigcup_{j=1}^r C_j,
\end{equation}
and
$$
\sum_{j=1}^r V(C_j)<\epsilon.
$$
original_proof:
Since $V(K)=0$,
$$
\int_C\psi_K(\mathbf{X})\,d\mathbf{X}=0
$$
if $C$ is any cube containing $K$. From this and the
definition of the integral, there is a $\delta>0$ such that if ${\bf
P}$ is any partition of $C$ with $\|{\bf P}\|\le\delta$ and $\sigma$
is any Riemann sum of $\psi_K$ over ${\bf P}$, then
\begin{equation}\label{eq:7.3.6}
0\le\sigma\le\epsilon.
\end{equation}
\newpage
\noindent
Now suppose that  ${\bf P}=\{C_1,C_2,\dots,C_k\}$ is a partition of $C$
into cubes with
\begin{equation}\label{eq:7.3.7}
\|{\bf P}\|<\min (\rho,\delta),
\end{equation}
and let $C_1$, $C_2$, \dots, $C_k$ be numbered so that $C_j\cap K\ne
\emptyset$ if $1\le j\le r$  and
$C_j\cap K=\emptyset$ if $r+1\le j\le k$. Then \eqref{eq:7.3.5} holds, and
a typical Riemann sum of $\psi_K$ over ${\bf P}$ is of the form
$$
\sigma=\sum_{j=1}^r\psi_K(\mathbf{X}_j)V(C_j)
$$
with $\mathbf{X}_j\in C_j$, $1\le j\le r$. In particular, we
can choose
$\mathbf{X}_j$ from $K$, so that $\psi_K(\mathbf{X}_j)=1$,  and
$$
\sigma=\sum_{j=1}^r V(C_j).
$$
Now \eqref{eq:7.3.6} and \eqref{eq:7.3.7} imply that $C_1$, $C_2$, \dots,
$C_r$ have the required properties.

```lean4
import Mathlib
import Aesop

set_option maxHeartbeats 0

open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat Filter

import Mathlib.Analysis.BoxIntegral.Content
import Mathlib.Analysis.BoxIntegral.Partition
import Mathlib.Analysis.BoxIntegral.Gauge

set_option maxHeartbeats 0

open Set Finset BoxIntegral BigOperators Topology Filter

variable {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι]

theorem covering_of_null_content_set (K : Set (ι → ℝ)) (hK_bounded : Bornology.IsBounded K)
    (hK_zero : HasZeroContent K) (ε : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) (ρ : ℝ) (hρ : 0 < ρ) :
    ∃ (s : Finset (Box ι)),
      (∀ J ∈ s, (J.Icc ∩ K).Nonempty) ∧
      (K ⊆ ⋃ J ∈ s, J.Icc) ∧
      (∑ J in s, J.volume) < ε ∧
      (∀ J ∈ s, ∀ i : ι, J.upper i - J.lower i < ρ) := by
  -- The informal proof relies on the Riemann integral definition of zero content.
  -- First, we establish a helper lemma that connects the integrability condition to
  -- a condition on the norm of partitions. This is what the informal proof assumes
  -- as "from the definition of the integral".
  have exists_upperNorm_lt_of_integrable {f : (ι → ℝ) → ℝ} {I : Box ι}
      (h_int : IntegrableOn f I) (h_integral_zero : ∫ x in I, f x = 0) :
      ∀ ε' > 0, ∃ δ > 0, ∀ (P : Partition I), P.upperNorm < δ →
      ∀ (tag : ∀ J ∈ P.boxes, J.Icc),
      abs (riemannSum f (P.toTaggedPartition tag)) < ε' := by
    intro ε' hε'
    have h_int_g : Integrable (I.indicator f) := h_int
    have h_has_integral : HasIntegral (I.indicator f) 0 := by
      rw [← h_integral_zero]
      exact h_int_g.hasIntegral
    have : Tendsto (fun π : TaggedPartition I => riemannSum (I.indicator f) π)
      (Gauge.toFilter I) (𝓝 0) := h_has_integral.tendsto
    have h_sum_eq : ∀ π, riemannSum (I.indicator f) π = riemannSum f π := fun π ↦
      riemannSum_congr_tag fun J _ => I.indicator_apply_eq_self.mpr
    rw [show (fun π => riemannSum (I.indicator f) π) = (fun π => riemannSum f π) by ext; simp [h_sum_eq]]
      at this
    rw [Metric.tendsto_nhds_nhds] at this
    specialize this ε' hε'
    rw [mem_toFilter_iff] at this
    obtain ⟨r, r_pos, hr_sub⟩ := this
    obtain ⟨δ, hδ_pos, hδ⟩ := I.isCompact_Icc.exists_uniform_gauge r r_pos
    use δ, hδ_pos
    intro P hP_norm tag
    apply hr_sub
    intro J hJ y hy
    specialize hδ (tag J hJ).1 (tag J hJ).2 y hy
    refine hδ.trans_le ?_
    calc
      dist y (tag J hJ) ≤ diam J.Icc := dist_le_diam_of_mem J.isConvex_Icc hy (tag J hJ).2
      _ ≤ P.upperNorm := Partition.diam_le_upperNorm P hJ
      _ < δ := hP_norm

  -- Let `f` be the characteristic function of `K`.
  let f : (ι → ℝ) → ℝ := indicator K fun _ => 1
  -- `HasZeroContent K` means it is integrable and its integral is zero.
  have ⟨hf_integrable, hf_integral_zero⟩ := (HasZeroContent.iff_integrable hK_bounded).mp hK_zero

  -- Since `K` is bounded, `closure K` is bounded, so it's contained in a box `C`.
  have hclK_bounded : Bornology.IsBounded (closure K) := hK_bounded.closure
  obtain ⟨C, hC_closure⟩ : ∃ (C : Box ι), closure K ⊆ C.Icc :=
    hclK_bounded.exists_box_containing
  have hK_sub_C : K ⊆ C.Icc := subset_trans subset_closure hC_closure

  have hf_integrable_on_C : IntegrableOn f C := by
    rw [IntegrableOn, C.indicator_indicator_of_subset hK_sub_C]
    exact hf_integrable

  have hC_integral_zero : ∫ x in C, f x = 0 := by
    rw [integral_indicator_of_subset hK_sub_C, hf_integral_zero]

  -- From our helper lemma, there exists `δ₁ > 0` such that for any partition `P`
  -- with norm less than `δ₁`, any Riemann sum is close to 0.
  obtain ⟨δ₁, hδ₁_pos, h_approx⟩ :=
    exists_upperNorm_lt_of_integrable hf_integrable_on_C hC_integral_zero ε hε

  -- Let `δ = min(ρ, δ₁)`.
  let δ := min ρ δ₁
  have hδ_pos : 0 < δ := lt_min hρ hδ₁_pos

  -- Now, construct a partition `P` of `C` with norm less than `δ`.
  obtain ⟨P, hP_norm⟩ : ∃ (P : Partition C), P.upperNorm < δ :=
    Partition.exists_upperNorm_lt C hδ_pos

  -- Let `s` be the set of boxes in `P` that intersect `K`.
  let s := P.boxes.filter (fun J ↦ (J.Icc ∩ K).Nonempty)

  -- 1. `∀ J ∈ s, (J.Icc ∩ K).Nonempty`. This holds by definition of `s`.
  have h_intersect : ∀ J ∈ s, (J.Icc ∩ K).Nonempty := fun J hJ ↦ (mem_filter.1 hJ).2

  -- 2. `K ⊆ ⋃ J ∈ s, J.Icc`
  have h_cover : K ⊆ ⋃ J ∈ s, J.Icc := by
    intro x hxK
    have hx_in_C : x ∈ C.Icc := hK_sub_C hxK
    rw [P.iUnion_Icc] at hx_in_C
    obtain ⟨J, hJ_in_P, hx_in_J⟩ := by simpa using hx_in_C
    use J
    have h_nonempty : (J.Icc ∩ K).Nonempty := ⟨x, hx_in_J, hxK⟩
    exact ⟨mem_filter.2 ⟨hJ_in_P, h_nonempty⟩, hx_in_J⟩

  -- For property 3 and 4, we construct a specific Riemann sum by choosing tags.
  let tags (J : Box ι) (hJ : J ∈ P.boxes) : J.Icc :=
    if h_nonempty : (J.Icc ∩ K).Nonempty then
      ⟨h_nonempty.choose, h_nonempty.choose_spec.1⟩
    else
      ⟨J.lower, J.lower_mem⟩

  let π := P.toTaggedPartition tags

  -- The norm of `P` is small enough, so the Riemann sum is small.
  have h_sum_approx : abs (riemannSum f π) < ε :=
    h_approx P (hP_norm.trans_le (min_le_right ..)) _

  -- Now let's analyze the Riemann sum.
  have h_sum_eq : riemannSum f π = ∑ J in s, J.volume := by
    rw [riemannSum_def, ← sum_add_sum_compl s]
    have sum_s : (∑ J in s, f (π.tag J) * J.volume) = ∑ J in s, J.volume := by
      apply sum_congr rfl
      intro J hJ
      simp only [mem_filter] at hJ
      have hJ_nonempty : (J.Icc ∩ K).Nonempty := hJ.2
      simp only [f, TaggedPartition.tag_apply, tags, dif_pos hJ_nonempty]
      rw [indicator_of_mem hJ_nonempty.choose_spec.2, one_mul]
    have sum_compl_s : (∑ J in P.boxes \ s, f (π.tag J) * J.volume) = 0 := by
      apply sum_eq_zero_of_forall_eq_zero
      intro J hJ_mem_compl
      simp only [mem_sdiff, mem_filter, not_and_not_right] at hJ_mem_compl
      have hJ_empty_inter : ¬ (J.Icc ∩ K).Nonempty := hJ_mem_compl.2
      simp only [f, TaggedPartition.tag_apply, tags, dif_neg hJ_empty_inter]
      rw [indicator_of_not_mem, zero_mul]
      intro h_lower_in_K
      apply hJ_empty_inter
      exact ⟨J.lower, J.lower_mem, h_lower_in_K⟩
    rw [sum_s, sum_compl_s, add_zero]

  -- 3. `(∑ J in s, Box.volume J) < ε`
  have h_sum_lt_ε : (∑ J in s, J.volume) < ε := by
    rwa [← h_sum_eq, abs_of_nonneg] at h_sum_approx
    exact sum_nonneg fun _ _ => Box.volume_nonneg

  -- 4. `∀ J ∈ s, ∀ i, J.upper i - J.lower i < ρ`
  have h_small_edges : ∀ J ∈ s, ∀ i : ι, J.upper i - J.lower i < ρ := by
    intro J hJ i
    have hJ_in_P : J ∈ P.boxes := (mem_filter.mp hJ).left
    calc
      J.upper i - J.lower i ≤ diam J.Icc := Box.edge_le_diam_Icc J i
      _ ≤ P.upperNorm := Partition.diam_le_upperNorm _ hJ_in_P
      _ < δ := hP_norm
      _ ≤ ρ := min_le_left ..

  -- Finally, we have all the required properties.
  exact ⟨s, h_intersect, h_cover, h_sum_lt_ε, h_small_edges⟩
```

  -- First, we establish a helper lemma that connects the integrability condition to
  -- a condition on the norm of partitions. This is what the informal proof assumes
  -- as "from the definition of the integral".

      (h_int : IntegrableOn f I) (h_integral_zero : ∫ x in I, f x = 0) :

      ∀ (tag : ∀ J ∈ P.boxes, J.Icc),

    have h_int_g : Integrable (I.indicator f) := h_int
    have h_has_integral : HasIntegral (I.indicator f) 0 := by

    have h_sum_eq : ∀ π, riemannSum (I.indicator f) π = riemannSum f π := fun π ↦
      riemannSum_congr_tag fun J _ => I.indicator_apply_eq_self.mpr
    rw [show (fun π => riemannSum (I.indicator f) π) = (fun π => riemannSum f π) by ext; simp [h_sum_eq]]
      at this
    rw [Metric.tendsto_nhds_nhds] at this
    specialize this ε' hε'
    rw [mem_toFilter_iff] at this
    obtain ⟨r, r_pos, hr_sub⟩ := this
    obtain ⟨δ, hδ_pos, hδ⟩ := I.isCompact_Icc.exists_uniform_gauge r r_pos
    use δ, hδ_pos

    apply hr_sub

  obtain ⟨C, hC_closure⟩ : ∃ (C : Box ι), closure K ⊆ C.Icc :=

    rw [riemannSum_def, ← sum_add_sum_compl s]

      have hJ_empty_inter : ¬ (J.Icc ∩ K).Nonempty := hJ_mem_compl.2

      rw [indicator_of_not_mem, zero_mul]
      intro h_lower_in_K
      apply hJ_empty_inter
      exact ⟨J.lower, J.lower_mem, h_lower_in_K⟩
    rw [sum_s, sum_compl_s, add_zero]

  have h_sum_lt_ε : (∑ J in s, J.volume) < ε := by
